doctor_description: Pericardial effusion measures up to 1.1 cm in thickness
validator_description: Pericardial effusion measures up to 8 mm in thickness
notes: Both describe a small pericardial effusion; doctor measures 11 mm, validator 8 mm. Neither measurement triggers additional workup at this size. Minor characterization difference.

doctor_description: Not mentioned
validator_description: Mild wall thickening and a small amount of secretions in the subsegmental bronchi; may represent bronchitis
notes: Validator adds mild subsegmental bronchial wall thickening suggesting bronchitis. Does not change management given coccidioidomycosis context on fluconazole.

doctor_description: Hypodense lesion with well-defined margins and fluid density, measuring up to 7 × 6 mm
validator_description: Hypodense lesion, measuring up to 4 mm; no further follow-up per ACR White Paper
notes: Significant size discrepancy (7×6 mm vs 4 mm) but both conclude no follow-up per ACR White Paper. Both sub-threshold for additional workup.

doctor_description: Not mentioned
validator_description: Hepatic steatosis (liver parenchyma density up to 30 HU)
notes: Validator identifies hepatic steatosis (30 HU). Doctor reports upper abdomen as unremarkable. Clinically relevant incidental finding that should be communicated.

doctor_description: Decreased vertebral body height at T5-T6 up to 30%
validator_description: Not mentioned
notes: Doctor reports up to 30% height loss at T5-T6 (potential compression fracture); validator does not mention it. In context of known DJD and instrumentation, management impact is limited.

doctor_description: Mild wall calcification; impression: mild atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta
validator_description: Not mentioned
notes: Doctor explicitly calls out mild aortic atherosclerosis in impression; validator omits it entirely. Common incidental finding with no direct management change; purely stylistic omission.

doctor_description: Enlarged 4L lymph node measures up to 13 mm in short axis; impression: mediastinal lymphadenopathy
validator_description: No masses or lymphadenopathy identified
management_impact: A 13 mm 4L lymph node in the context of a known solid 9 mm pulmonary nodule is clinically significant and would influence staging, potentially prompting earlier PET/CT or tissue sampling.
notes: Doctor identifies a pathologically enlarged 4L lymph node (13 mm short axis) that validator explicitly denies. In a patient with a solid lung nodule requiring follow-up, mediastinal lymphadenopathy is a major finding that would change management.

doctor_description: Small hiatal hernia with partial herniation of the gastric cardia into the posterior inferior mediastinum
validator_description: Not mentioned
notes: Doctor reports a small hiatal hernia; validator does not. Common incidental finding that does not change radiological management.

doctor_description: Mild thickening of the walls of the subsegmental bronchi, with mild cylindrical dilatation of the lumina in lung bases bilaterally; impression: cylindrical bronchiectasis
validator_description: Not mentioned
notes: Doctor identifies cylindrical bronchiectasis in bilateral lung bases; validator omits entirely. Mild bronchiectasis is a clinically meaningful chronic airways finding that should be communicated.

doctor_description: Decreased height of the T12 vertebral body by 21%
validator_description: Not mentioned as height loss; only decreased bone density reported
notes: Doctor specifically notes T12 height decrease of 21% (mild compression deformity); validator only reports decreased bone density. Relevant finding in context of demineralization, but no acute management change.

doctor_description: Right pulmonary artery 2.7 cm, left pulmonary artery 2.5 cm
validator_description: Not mentioned; only main pulmonary artery dilation reported
notes: Doctor provides branch PA measurements; validator omits. Both agree on main PA dilation at 3.0 cm. Branch measurements are supplementary and do not change clinical conclusion.

doctor_description: Not mentioned
validator_description: Moderate degenerative changes of the shoulder joints bilaterally
notes: Validator notes moderate bilateral shoulder joint degenerative changes; doctor does not. Common incidental finding; no management impact.

doctor_description: Mixed emphysema in the lungs, with air bullae measuring up to 1.5 cm
validator_description: Diffuse multiple pulmonary cysts with thin walls and upper lobe predominance; consider pulmonary Langerhans cell histiocytosis
management_impact: Validator's characterization as diffuse thin-walled cysts with upper lobe predominance raises the possibility of PLCH, which would require further workup (PFTs, possible biopsy or CT follow-up). Doctor's characterization as emphysematous bullae does not prompt additional evaluation.
notes: Significant characterization difference: doctor calls these emphysematous bullae; validator describes thin-walled cysts with upper lobe predominance and raises PLCH. This distinction materially changes the differential diagnosis and potential management.

finding_category: Coronary artery calcification — grade and extent
doctor_description: Mild coronary artery calcification in the left anterior descending and circumflex arteries
validator_description: Coronary artery calcifications in the left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate
notes: Doctor grades CAC as mild involving LAD and LCx only; validator grades as moderate and adds RCA. Both recommend PCP referral. The severity and vessel extent differences are clinically relevant but both reach the same management recommendation.

doctor_description: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation
validator_description: Severe degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with ossification of the anterior longitudinal ligament
notes: Doctor grades as moderate with osteophytes; validator grades as severe with DISH pattern. Different severity grading and specific pattern recognition but no acute management change.

doctor_description: Not mentioned
validator_description: Wall thickening and secretion of the segmental and subsegmental bronchi; may represent bronchitis
notes: Validator adds bronchial wall thickening with secretions suggesting bronchitis; doctor does not mention this. Does not change management in this clinical context.

doctor_description: Decreased bone density in the L1 vertebral body to +149 HU; Schmorl's node at L2
validator_description: Not specifically mentioned (spine described as severe DJD with DISH)
notes: Doctor provides specific HU measurement for L1 and notes Schmorl's node; validator focuses on severe DJD/DISH pattern. +149 HU is borderline normal so omission is not clinically significant.

doctor_description: Not mentioned; upper abdomen reported as unremarkable
validator_description: Nonobstructing calculus (2 mm) in the upper pole of the right kidney
notes: Validator identifies a 2 mm nonobstructing right renal calculus; doctor reports upper abdomen as unremarkable. Small nonobstructing renal calculi typically do not require immediate intervention but are clinically relevant incidental findings.

doctor_description: Stable vs October 07, 2025; likely postinfectious scar/granuloma; no follow-up required or repeat CT in 12-24 months
validator_description: Unchanged vs 10/07/2025; compared with 12/22/2023: decreased in size, no cavitation, fewer micronodules; prior subsolid nodules resolved; Fleischner not applicable; residual post-infectious nodule
notes: Validator provides richer longitudinal comparison with 12/22/2023 study, documenting resolution of cavitation and size decrease — strengthening the benign post-infectious interpretation. Both conclude similarly with no meaningful management difference.

doctor_description: Pericardial effusion measures up to 6 mm in thickness; impression: minimal pericardial effusion
validator_description: No significant pericardial effusion
notes: Doctor reports a 6 mm pericardial effusion; validator states no significant pericardial effusion. At 6 mm this is trace/minimal; characterization difference is minor and does not change management.

doctor_description: Not mentioned; only aortic valve calcification reported
validator_description: Aortic and mitral valve calcification
notes: Validator identifies mitral valve calcification in addition to aortic valve calcification; doctor mentions only aortic valve. Clinically relevant incidental finding without immediate management impact.

doctor_description: Healing fracture of the left 5th rib
validator_description: Not mentioned
notes: Doctor reports a healing left 5th rib fracture; validator omits. Both report non-united right 6th and 7th rib fractures. Healing fracture is a relevant finding that should be documented.

anatomical_location: Para-aortic region, lesser omentum, visceral nodes
doctor_description: Increased number of visible visceral lymph nodes; para-aortic lymph nodes enlarged up to 1.1 cm in short axis; impression: retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy
validator_description: Not mentioned
management_impact: In a patient with a known enlarging gastric fundus mass (possible GIST) and mycobacterial lung disease, para-aortic lymphadenopathy (1.1 cm) could indicate lymph node involvement from the gastric mass or systemic disease, affecting staging and management.
notes: Doctor reports para-aortic lymphadenopathy up to 1.1 cm in context of an enlarging gastric fundus mass; validator completely omits abdominal lymph node findings. Significant omission given the suspicious abdominal mass.

doctor_description: Not mentioned
validator_description: Pulmonary emphysema is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in LDCT lung cancer screening program if patient meets eligibility criteria.
management_impact: LDCT screening recommendation if criteria met
notes: Validator includes LDCT screening recommendation for emphysema (QMM23). Patient 57M, current smoker — eligible. Doctor omits recommendation.

doctor_description: Mild bilateral reticular opacities
validator_description: Not mentioned
notes: Doctor notes mild bilateral reticular opacities; validator does not mention these. Clinically noteworthy in context of mycobacterial disease but does not change management given stable imaging.
